perf.regime: One program per (request, qo_head): load the query head vector into registers, stream the request's paged KV in BLOCK_N=64 chunks with the flash (online) softmax. The page indirection (j -> block_table[b, j//block_size], j%block_size) lives INSIDE the chunk loop. GQA is free (each program owns one qo head, reading exactly its mapped kv head h//group). Memory-bound on the paged KV read at decode (1 q-token/request x large KV history); no causal mask (the new token attends to all past positions). num_warps=4, num_stages=2.

notes: The vLLM paged_attention / flashinfer BatchDecodeWithPagedKVCache shape. BLOCK_D=next_pow2(D) (64 or 128 for common head dims) and GROUP=H_q//H_kv are tl.constexpr derived from dispatch args (cached) -- NOT user specialization_knobs. BLOCK_N=64 is the flash-chunk size; the flash reduction is exact for any chunk size so this is a pure perf knob (autotune follow-up). Portable (pure tl.dot-free manual FMA + tl.exp, no vendor intrinsics); the AMD gfx942 card is the port-across-arch follow-up (the issue's primary target). PREFILL (varlen) is a SEPARATE op (paged_attention_prefill) -- a follow-up with a different q shape and a varlen flash kernel.
